context.pr_title: Sanitize NaN, Infinite, -Infinite causing error when saving as PostgreSQL JSON
context.pr_body: ## What type of PR is this? - [x] Bug Fix ## Description When running query that output NaN, Infinite, or -Infinite, redash executor crashes with the error "Out of range float values are not JSON compliant" like below. Redash does not output any rows when the error was occurred. ``` [2025-02-19 04:48:49,040][PID:593][ERROR][rq.worker] [Job 6cfa1d1c-2a32-48c6-8bf4-0cd8f8ba6f32]: exception raised while executing (redash.tasks.queries.execution.execute_query) Traceback (most recent call last): ... File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/rq/job.py", line 1280, in perform self._result = self._execute() File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/rq/job.py", line 1317, in _execute result = self.func(*self.args, **self.kwargs) File "/app/redash/tasks/queries/execution.py", line 309, in execute_query ).run() File "/app/redash/tasks/queries/execution.py", line 253, in run updated_query_ids = models.Query.update_latest_result(query_result) File "/app/redash/models/__init__.py", line 738, in update_latest_result for q in queries: File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/sqlalchemy/orm/query.py", line 3534, in __iter__ self.session._autoflush() ... sqlalchemy.exc.StatementError: (raised as a result of Query-invoked autoflush; consider using a session.no_autoflush block if this flush is occurring prematurely) (builtins.ValueError) Out of range float values are not JSON compliant [SQL: INSERT INTO query_results (org_id, data_source_id, query_hash, query, data, runtime, retrieved_at) VALUES (%(org_id)s, %(data_source_id)s, %(query_hash)s, %(query)s, %(data)s, %(runtime)s, %(retrieved_at)s) RETURNING query_results.id] ... ``` The error occurs because JSON(PostgreSQL JSON type) does not support NaN, Infinite, -Infinite. This PR fixes the issue by sanitize NaN, Infinite, -Infinite value to None. This is the save behavior that JavaScript JSON.stringify() does. We can reproduce the issue by running following query on BigQuery or PostgreSQL. BigQuery: ``` select 1.0, cast('NaN' as float64), cast('Infinity' as float64), cast('-Infinity' as float64) ``` PostgreSQL: ``` select 1.0::float, 'NaN'::float, 'Infinity'::float, '-Infinity'::float ``` The issue was introduced by #6685 which removes simplejson. Before #6685 , NaN / Inf was converted to null by simplejson with ignore_nan=True. This PR change behavior for NaN/Inf as same as before #6685 . ## How is this tested? - [x] Unit tests (pytest, jest) - [x] Manually Unit test was added. Also, we can confirm by running the query on the description. Before this PR: After this PR:

import datetime

from redash import models
from redash.utils import utcnow
from tests import BaseTestCase


class QueryResultTest(BaseTestCase):
    def test_get_latest_returns_none_if_not_found(self):
        found_query_result = models.QueryResult.get_latest(self.factory.data_source, "SELECT 1", 60)
        self.assertIsNone(found_query_result)

    def test_get_latest_returns_when_found(self):
        qr = self.factory.create_query_result()
        found_query_result = models.QueryResult.get_latest(qr.data_source, qr.query_text, 60)

        self.assertEqual(qr, found_query_result)

    def test_get_latest_doesnt_return_query_from_different_data_source(self):
        qr = self.factory.create_query_result()
        data_source = self.factory.create_data_source()
        found_query_result = models.QueryResult.get_latest(data_source, qr.query_text, 60)

        self.assertIsNone(found_query_result)

    def test_get_latest_doesnt_return_if_ttl_expired(self):
        yesterday = utcnow() - datetime.timedelta(days=1)
        qr = self.factory.create_query_result(retrieved_at=yesterday)

        found_query_result = models.QueryResult.get_latest(qr.data_source, qr.query_text, max_age=60)

        self.assertIsNone(found_query_result)

    def test_get_latest_returns_if_ttl_not_expired(self):
        yesterday = utcnow() - datetime.timedelta(seconds=30)
        qr = self.factory.create_query_result(retrieved_at=yesterday)

        found_query_result = models.QueryResult.get_latest(qr.data_source, qr.query_text, max_age=120)

        self.assertEqual(found_query_result, qr)

    def test_get_latest_returns_the_most_recent_result(self):
        yesterday = utcnow() - datetime.timedelta(seconds=30)
        self.factory.create_query_result(retrieved_at=yesterday)
        qr = self.factory.create_query_result()

        found_query_result = models.QueryResult.get_latest(qr.data_source, qr.query_text, 60)

        self.assertEqual(found_query_result.id, qr.id)

    def test_get_latest_returns_the_last_cached_result_for_negative_ttl(self):
        yesterday = utcnow() + datetime.timedelta(days=-100)
        self.factory.create_query_result(retrieved_at=yesterday)

        yesterday = utcnow() + datetime.timedelta(days=-1)
        qr = self.factory.create_query_result(retrieved_at=yesterday)
        found_query_result = models.QueryResult.get_latest(qr.data_source, qr.query_text, -1)

        self.assertEqual(found_query_result.id, qr.id)

    def test_store_result_does_not_modify_query_update_at(self):
        original_updated_at = utcnow() - datetime.timedelta(hours=1)
        query = self.factory.create_query(updated_at=original_updated_at)

        models.QueryResult.store_result(
            query.org_id,
            query.data_source,
            query.query_hash,
            query.query_text,
            {},
            0,
            utcnow(),
        )

        self.assertEqual(original_updated_at, query.updated_at)

output.pr_title: Sanitize NaN, Infinite, -Infinite causing error when saving as PostgreSQL JSON
output.pr_body:
Bug fix: Redash executor crashes when query output includes NaN/Infinity/-Infinity because PostgreSQL JSON/JSONB (and Redash’s JSON serialization settings) do not allow out-of-range float values. Stacktrace shows failure during INSERT into query_results.data.

Root cause: after #6685 removed simplejson (which used ignore_nan=True), Redash now uses `redash.utils.json_dumps` with `allow_nan=False`, which raises `ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant` when encoding data containing NaN/Inf.

Desired behavior: sanitize NaN/Inf/-Inf to `None` (JSON null), matching JavaScript `JSON.stringify` behavior. Add unit tests and confirm manually with example BigQuery/PostgreSQL queries.
output.masca_optimized:
### MASCA (optimized for this PR)

#### M — Motivation
- Prevent query execution workers from crashing when result sets contain IEEE-754 non-finite floats (`NaN`, `Infinity`, `-Infinity`).
- Restore pre-#6685 behavior (simplejson `ignore_nan=True`), ensuring query results are still persisted and returned to users.

#### A — Approach
- Sanitize query result payloads before they are serialized for storage in Postgres.
- Convert any non-finite float values recursively (in dicts/lists/tuples) to `None`.
- Keep existing JSON encoding constraint (`allow_nan=False`) so invalid JSON is never produced; instead, data is made compliant.
- Place sanitization at a shared boundary used by DB persistence (best: right before `json.dumps` in `json_dumps`, or inside `JSONText.process_bind_param`).

Recommended placement:
- **Primary**: `redash.utils.json_dumps` — guarantees all JSON serialization done by Redash (DB storage, caching, API responses where used) becomes compliant and consistent.
- **Alternative / defense-in-depth**: `redash.models.types.JSONText.process_bind_param` — ensures DB writes never fail even if some other code bypasses `json_dumps`.

#### S — Scope
- Server-side only (Python). No DB migrations.
- Add unit tests validating:
  - `json_dumps` produces valid JSON and converts NaN/Inf to null.
  - Storing `QueryResult.data` with NaN/Inf does not raise and round-trips to Python `None`.

#### C — Complexity / Risks
- Must handle nested structures and mixed types efficiently.
- Ensure we don’t mutate input structures in surprising ways (prefer deep transformation or carefully documented in-place behavior).
- Ensure sanitization does not affect legitimate numeric values.
- Consider that `decimal.Decimal` conversion to float can create `inf` for huge values; sanitization should handle that too.

#### A — Acceptance Criteria
- Query execution no longer fails when results contain NaN/Inf/-Inf; results are saved and returned.
- Serialized JSON contains `null` instead of `NaN`/`Infinity` tokens.
- Unit tests cover sanitization behavior and DB persistence path.

files_to_modify.reason: Contains `json_dumps` used by `JSONText` and other components. Add recursive sanitization of non-finite floats before calling `json.dumps` (keeping `allow_nan=False`). This is the most central fix point.
`JSONText.process_bind_param` is the DB boundary for QueryResult.data. Optionally add defense-in-depth sanitization here (or ensure it uses updated `json_dumps`) so Postgres inserts never fail.
Add/extend unit tests to validate storing QueryResult.data containing NaN/Inf does not error and is persisted as null/None on load.

If a utils test module exists/should be added: add focused unit tests for `json_dumps`/sanitization (pure function tests, no DB). If not present currently, create it under tests/ for serialization behavior.

functions_to_modify.reason: Currently sets `allow_nan=False` which raises ValueError when NaN/Inf appear. Modify to pre-sanitize payload (convert NaN/Inf/-Inf to None) before dumping.
This is where QueryResult.data is serialized for storage. Ensure it serializes sanitized JSON (either by relying on updated `json_dumps` or adding explicit sanitization here for safety).

Not strictly required if sanitization is centralized, but it’s the call site where raw runner output is attached to `QueryResult.data`. If maintainers prefer narrower scope, sanitization could be applied here to only affect stored query results.

output.analysis_summary:
The crash occurs during SQLAlchemy flush/INSERT of `QueryResult.data` because `JSONText.process_bind_param` uses `redash.utils.json_dumps`, which uses `json.dumps(..., allow_nan=False)`. When query runner outputs non-finite floats, Python raises `ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant`, aborting query execution and preventing any rows from being returned.

The most robust fix is to sanitize non-finite floats to `None` before JSON serialization. Centralizing this in `redash.utils.json_dumps` ensures consistent behavior across all JSON serialization paths. Optionally, add defense-in-depth in `JSONText.process_bind_param` or sanitize specifically at `QueryResult.store_result` if a narrower behavioral change is desired. Tests should cover both the serializer behavior and the QueryResult persistence round-trip.

agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
Sanitize NaN, Infinite, -Infinite causing error when saving as PostgreSQL JSON

## PR Body
Bug fix: Redash executor crashes when query output includes NaN/Infinity/-Infinity because PostgreSQL JSON/JSONB (and Redash’s JSON serialization settings) do not allow out-of-range float values. Stacktrace shows failure during INSERT into query_results.data.

Root cause: after #6685 removed simplejson (which used ignore_nan=True), Redash now uses `redash.utils.json_dumps` with `allow_nan=False`, which raises `ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant` when encoding data containing NaN/Inf.

Desired behavior: sanitize NaN/Inf/-Inf to `None` (JSON null), matching JavaScript `JSON.stringify` behavior. Add unit tests and confirm manually with example BigQuery/PostgreSQL queries.

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
### MASCA (optimized for this PR)

#### M — Motivation
- Prevent query execution workers from crashing when result sets contain IEEE-754 non-finite floats (`NaN`, `Infinity`, `-Infinity`).
- Restore pre-#6685 behavior (simplejson `ignore_nan=True`), ensuring query results are still persisted and returned to users.

#### A — Approach
- Sanitize query result payloads before they are serialized for storage in Postgres.
- Convert any non-finite float values recursively (in dicts/lists/tuples) to `None`.
- Keep existing JSON encoding constraint (`allow_nan=False`) so invalid JSON is never produced; instead, data is made compliant.
- Place sanitization at a shared boundary used by DB persistence (best: right before `json.dumps` in `json_dumps`, or inside `JSONText.process_bind_param`).

Recommended placement:
- **Primary**: `redash.utils.json_dumps` — guarantees all JSON serialization done by Redash (DB storage, caching, API responses where used) becomes compliant and consistent.
- **Alternative / defense-in-depth**: `redash.models.types.JSONText.process_bind_param` — ensures DB writes never fail even if some other code bypasses `json_dumps`.

#### S — Scope
- Server-side only (Python). No DB migrations.
- Add unit tests validating:
  - `json_dumps` produces valid JSON and converts NaN/Inf to null.
  - Storing `QueryResult.data` with NaN/Inf does not raise and round-trips to Python `None`.

#### C — Complexity / Risks
- Must handle nested structures and mixed types efficiently.
- Ensure we don’t mutate input structures in surprising ways (prefer deep transformation or carefully documented in-place behavior).
- Ensure sanitization does not affect legitimate numeric values.
- Consider that `decimal.Decimal` conversion to float can create `inf` for huge values; sanitization should handle that too.

#### A — Acceptance Criteria
- Query execution no longer fails when results contain NaN/Inf/-Inf; results are saved and returned.
- Serialized JSON contains `null` instead of `NaN`/`Infinity` tokens.
- Unit tests cover sanitization behavior and DB persistence path.


## Files to Modify
- redash/utils/__init__.py: Contains `json_dumps` used by `JSONText` and other components. Add recursive sanitization of non-finite floats before calling `json.dumps` (keeping `allow_nan=False`). This is the most central fix point.
- redash/models/types.py: `JSONText.process_bind_param` is the DB boundary for QueryResult.data. Optionally add defense-in-depth sanitization here (or ensure it uses updated `json_dumps`) so Postgres inserts never fail.
- tests/models/test_query_results.py: Add/extend unit tests to validate storing QueryResult.data containing NaN/Inf does not error and is persisted as null/None on load.
- tests/test_utils.py: If a utils test module exists/should be added: add focused unit tests for `json_dumps`/sanitization (pure function tests, no DB). If not present currently, create it under tests/ for serialization behavior.

## Functions to Modify
- redash.utils.json_dumps (redash/utils/__init__.py): Currently sets `allow_nan=False` which raises ValueError when NaN/Inf appear. Modify to pre-sanitize payload (convert NaN/Inf/-Inf to None) before dumping.
- redash.models.types.JSONText.process_bind_param (redash/models/types.py): This is where QueryResult.data is serialized for storage. Ensure it serializes sanitized JSON (either by relying on updated `json_dumps` or adding explicit sanitization here for safety).
- QueryResult.store_result (redash/models/__init__.py): Not strictly required if sanitization is centralized, but it’s the call site where raw runner output is attached to `QueryResult.data`. If maintainers prefer narrower scope, sanitization could be applied here to only affect stored query results.

## Analysis Summary
The crash occurs during SQLAlchemy flush/INSERT of `QueryResult.data` because `JSONText.process_bind_param` uses `redash.utils.json_dumps`, which uses `json.dumps(..., allow_nan=False)`. When query runner outputs non-finite floats, Python raises `ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant`, aborting query execution and preventing any rows from being returned.

The most robust fix is to sanitize non-finite floats to `None` before JSON serialization. Centralizing this in `redash.utils.json_dumps` ensures consistent behavior across all JSON serialization paths. Optionally, add defense-in-depth in `JSONText.process_bind_param` or sanitize specifically at `QueryResult.store_result` if a narrower behavioral change is desired. Tests should cover both the serializer behavior and the QueryResult persistence round-trip.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Add recursive sanitization of non-finite floats (NaN/Infinity/-Infinity) to None before JSON encoding, while keeping allow_nan=False
steps.reason: `json.dumps(..., allow_nan=False)` raises `ValueError` when the payload contains NaN/Inf. Centralizing a recursive sanitizer in `json_dumps` ensures any Redash code path that relies on this helper (DB persistence, caching, API responses where used) produces JSON-compliant output and matches JavaScript `JSON.stringify` behavior (non-finite numbers -> null).
steps.side_effects: Changes serialization semantics globally for callers of `json_dumps`: NaN/Inf/-Inf will silently become `null` instead of erroring. This may affect any downstream logic that previously depended on the exception or on preserving NaN/Inf tokens (which are invalid JSON anyway). Add/adjust unit tests that assert conversion and ensure performance is acceptable for large result payloads.

steps.operation: Ensure DB-bound JSON serialization path cannot write non-finite floats by relying on updated json_dumps (and/or adding a final defensive sanitize pass before returning)
steps.reason: This is the SQLAlchemy boundary used to serialize `QueryResult.data` into Postgres JSON/JSONB-compatible text. Even if other code bypasses `json_dumps`, this method must guarantee JSON compliance so inserts/updates never crash during flush.
steps.side_effects: If additional sanitization is added here, it may duplicate work already done by `json_dumps` (minor CPU overhead). Behavioral change affects all columns using `JSONText`: persisted JSON will contain nulls for non-finite floats rather than raising. Requires confirming round-trip decode produces Python `None`.

steps.operation: Sanitize runner output before storing it into QueryResult.data (if maintainers prefer a narrower or defense-in-depth fix at the query-results boundary)
steps.reason: `store_result` is where raw query-runner results are attached to the model before persistence. Applying sanitization here provides defense-in-depth and makes the behavior explicit for query results, even if other storage paths later change. This also helps keep `QueryResult.data` consistently free of non-finite floats in memory (not only at serialization time).
steps.side_effects: May increase memory usage and CPU due to deep transformation of result payloads at store time (in addition to serialization). Could slightly change in-memory behavior for callers that inspect `QueryResult.data` prior to commit. If sanitization is already fully handled in `json_dumps`/`JSONText`, this step might be redundant—ensure tests reflect intended single-source-of-truth to avoid double-processing.
